procedure.name: Atomic Red Team validation for T1201
procedure.description: Automated emulation procedure for T1201 executed via Invoke-AtomicRedTeam inside the Metatron lab environment.

execution.command_line: ['docker', 'run', '--rm', '--name', 'seraph-sandbox-bab9865b2df1', '--network', 'none', '--cap-drop', 'ALL', '--cap-add', 'SETUID', '--cap-add', 'SETGID', '--cap-add', 'CHOWN', '--cap-add', 'DAC_OVERRIDE', '--cap-add', 'FOWNER', '--cap-add', 'SYS_PTRACE', '--cap-add', 'NET_RAW', '--security-opt', 'no-new-privileges:false', '--tmpfs', '/tmp:exec,size=512m', '-v', '/home/byron/Downloads/Metatron-triune-outbound-gate/atomic-red-team:/opt/atomic-red-team:ro', '-v', '/home/byron/Downloads/Metatron-triune-outbound-gate/tools/invoke-atomicredteam:/opt/invoke-atomicredteam:ro', '-v', '/home/byron/Downloads/Metatron-triune-outbound-gate/tools/powershell:/opt/pwsh:ro', 'seraph-sandbox-tools:latest', '/opt/pwsh/pwsh', '-NonInteractive', '-NoProfile', '-Command', 'Import-Module \'/opt/invoke-atomicredteam/Invoke-AtomicRedTeam.psd1\' -ErrorAction Stop; $env:PathToAtomicsFolder=\'/opt/atomic-red-team/atomics\'; try { bash -lc "if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive; apt-get update -y >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; apt-get install -y inetutils-telnet ldap-utils lsof mlocate smbclient samba-common-bin dnsutils >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi; if ! command -v b64encode >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v base64 >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf \'#!/usr/bin/env bash\\nbase64 "$@"\\n\' > /usr/local/bin/b64encode && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/b64encode; fi" } catch { Write-Host \'[WARN] dependency bootstrap failed\'; }; try { Invoke-AtomicTest T1201 -PathToAtomicsFolder \'/opt/atomic-red-team/atomics\' -TestNumbers 1 -GetPrereqs -ErrorAction Continue } catch { Write-Host \'[WARN] GetPrereqs failed for T1201\'; }; Invoke-AtomicTest T1201 -PathToAtomicsFolder \'/opt/atomic-red-team/atomics\' -TestNumbers 1']

command: Import-Module '/opt/invoke-atomicredteam/Invoke-AtomicRedTeam.psd1' -ErrorAction Stop; $env:PathToAtomicsFolder='/opt/atomic-red-team/atomics'; try { bash -lc "if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive; apt-get update -y >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; apt-get install -y inetutils-telnet ldap-utils lsof mlocate smbclient samba-common-bin dnsutils >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi; if ! command -v b64encode >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v base64 >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\nbase64 "$@"\n' > /usr/local/bin/b64encode && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/b64encode; fi" } catch { Write-Host '[WARN] dependency bootstrap failed'; }; try { Invoke-AtomicTest T1201 -PathToAtomicsFolder '/opt/atomic-red-team/atomics' -TestNumbers 1 -GetPrereqs -ErrorAction Continue } catch { Write-Host '[WARN] GetPrereqs failed for T1201'; }; Invoke-AtomicTest T1201 -PathToAtomicsFolder '/opt/atomic-red-team/atomics' -TestNumbers 1

GetPrereq's for: T1201-1 Examine password complexity policy - Ubuntu
No Preqs Defined
PathToAtomicsFolder = /opt/atomic-red-team/atomics

Executing test: T1201-1 Examine password complexity policy - Ubuntu
# /etc/pam.d/common-password - password-related modules common to all services
#
#between Debian 11 and older releases replace "yescrypt" with "sha512"
#for compatibility .  The "obscure" option replaces the old
#`OBSCURE_CHECKS_ENAB' option in login.defs.  See the pam_unix manpage
#for other options.
# As of pam 1.0.1-6, this file is managed by pam-auth-update by default.
# To take advantage of this, it is recommended that you configure any
# local modules either before or after the default block, and use
# pam-auth-update to manage selection of other modules.  See
# pam-auth-update(8) for details.
# here are the per-package modules (the "Primary" block)
password	[success=1 default=ignore]	pam_unix.so obscure yescrypt
# here's the fallback if no module succeeds
password	requisite			pam_deny.so
# prime the stack with a positive return value if there isn't one already;
# this avoids us returning an error just because nothing sets a success code
# since the modules above will each just jump around
password	required			pam_permit.so
# and here are more per-package modules (the "Additional" block)
# end of pam-auth-update config
#
# This file is included from other service-specific PAM config files,
# and should contain a list of modules that define the services to be
#11.  Without this option, the default is Unix crypt.  Prior releases
# Explanation of pam_unix options:
# used to change user passwords.  The default is pam_unix.
# The "yescrypt" option enables
#hashed passwords using the yescrypt algorithm, introduced in Debian
#used the option "sha512"; if a shadow password hash will be shared
Exit code: 0
Done executing test: T1201-1 Examine password complexity policy - Ubuntu

Executing test: T1087.001-2 View sudoers access
#
# This file MUST be edited with the 'visudo' command as root.
#
# Please consider adding local content in /etc/sudoers.d/ instead of
# directly modifying this file.
#
# See the man page for details on how to write a sudoers file.
#
Defaults	env_reset
Defaults	mail_badpass
Defaults	secure_path="/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"

osquery.name: t1201_sys_info
osquery.query_text: SELECT hostname, cpu_type, physical_memory, hardware_vendor, hardware_model FROM system_info;

osquery.name: t1201_net_enum
osquery.query_text: SELECT interface, address, mask, type FROM interface_addresses WHERE interface != 'lo';
